unresolved.title: Moto Rush Reborn Review: Born To Be Wiiiiild
unresolved.summary:
You know the saying that “South Africa is a movie”? Well, Mzansi is a bit of a movie star too. We had the Avengers doing things in Johannesburg (now if only they’d repair all the roads they had clearly wrecked), and multiple films use Clarence Drive as a backdrop. unresolved.title: Forget RGB: Three Pieces of Hardware That Let Wood Do the Talking
unresolved.summary:
If your PC isn’t visible from space, you clearly aren’t trying hard enough. Put the word gaming into the mix, and you’re guaranteed to have some sort of RGB thrown in. But what if we left that aside? What if we went back to au naturel and let the materials do the talking? Instead of trying to make our desks brighter, let’s make them warmer. unresolved.title: Epomaker Split 65 Keyboard Review: Splitting My Thoughts on Comfort
unresolved.summary:
There has definitely been an overall upgrade in keyboards over the past few years. A lot of the brands we know and love have taken it upon themselves to add hot-swappable mechanical keys and sound-dampening gaskets to some of their more basic boards. This means great keyboards are not just devices reserved for those willing to risk an unknown brand, or modders who know all the little secrets any regular Joe wouldn’t.
